name: Peppered Farmhouse Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. (175°C.)
2. Remove rinds and bones from bacon and pork.
3. Stretch the bacon on a board using the back of a knife until it is almost double in length. Line the bottom and sides of a 5-cup terrine or souffle dish with bacon.
4. Mince the shoulder pork, liver, onion, and garlic. Cut veal into 1/2-inch pieces.
5. In a bowl, combine the pork shoulder, liver, veal, onion, and garlic.
6. Stir in egg, salt, peppercorns, herbs, and brandy; mix thoroughly.
7. Spoon the mixture into the bacon-lined dish and smooth the surface. Cover tightly with foil.
8. Place the dish into a roasting pan half filled with hot water. Bake in the preheated oven for 2 hours.
9. Allow to cool for 30 minutes. Top with a plate and place a heavy weight on the plate. Cool completely, then refrigerate overnight.
10. Turn out onto a serving plate. Garnish with additional peppercorns and bay leaf, if desired, and serve with bread.
